name: Low-Fat Pumpkin Flan (Lacto)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 300°F.
2. In a saucepan, cook the sugar over medium-low heat until it begins to caramelize and turns golden and liquid. Stir constantly until all the sugar is melted and golden.
3. Quickly pour the caramelized sugar into a 9-inch round cake or pie pan, tipping the pan so that the sugar coats the entire bottom. The sugar will harden in about 20 seconds, but remain very hot. Do not touch.
4. In a medium bowl, mix together the cloves, ginger, and cinnamon.
5. Add evaporated skim milk, egg, egg whites, and brown sugar substitute to the dry ingredients. Mix with an electric mixer on low until the sugar substitute is dissolved.
6. Add the pumpkin mixture and beat at a low speed until smooth.
7. Pour the pumpkin mixture into the pan with the caramelized sugar.
8. Place the pan in a larger, shallow pan. Fill the larger pan with hot water to a depth of 1 inch.
9. Place the setup in the preheated oven and bake until a knife inserted near the center comes out clean (about 1 hour).
10. Remove the pan from the oven and the water, then let it cool. Cover and chill for at least 3 hours.
11. To serve, loosen the edges with a spatula. Place a larger serving dish on top of the pan. Invert the flan onto the plate. Any sauce remaining in the bottom of the pan should be poured back into the serving dish.
